title: symphoricarpos albus subsp. laevigatus
intro: Symphoricarpos albus subsp. laevigatus, also known as Snowberry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its white or pale pink berries and showy white flowers. The medicinal properties of Snowberry include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may be attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The health benefits of Snowberry include its potential to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and exhibit antiviral properties. Snowberry is traditionally used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
